name: software-engineer
description: Builds and refactors code safely; prefers small diffs, clear validation steps, and testable outcomes.
capabilities: Guides developers to create small, focused code changes that are easy to review
Asks clarifying questions when requirements are ambiguous before implementing
Suggests using existing repository patterns and utilities instead of adding new dependencies
Proposes validation steps like unit tests or build commands when behavior changes
Instructs to avoid destructive actions unless explicitly requested by the user
author: ACSKamloops
securitySummary: All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill contains only guidance for safe code development practices. The source_url field is metadata, not a network call. Words like 'code', 'refactor', and 'testable' were incorrectly flagged by pattern matching. No actual security risks found.
valueStatement: Code changes often introduce bugs and regressions. This skill guides developers through safe code building and refactoring by preferring minimal, reviewable changes and clear validation steps.
limitations: Does not execute code or run tests automatically
Does not have access to specific project coding standards or style guides
Does not generate comprehensive test suites from scratch
Provides guidance but does not make architectural decisions

useCases.targetUser: Developers working with legacy or complex codebases
useCases.title: Safe Code Refactoring
useCases.description: Refactor legacy code with minimal, reviewable changes. The skill breaks down large refactors into smaller steps, asks clarifying questions about ambiguous requirements, and suggests validation tests at each step.

useCases.targetUser: Feature developers seeking consistent code quality
useCases.title: Feature Implementation Guidance
useCases.description: Implement new features following existing patterns. The skill guides developers to use repository utilities, prefer small diffs, and propose quick validation steps to confirm correct behavior.

useCases.targetUser: Developers preparing pull requests for review
useCases.title: Code Review Preparation
useCases.description: Prepare code changes for review. The skill ensures changes are minimal, well-scoped, and include appropriate validation steps to make the review process smoother.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Refactor Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Refactor the function [FUNCTION_NAME] to improve readability while maintaining its current behavior.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to refactor a specific function with clear scope

promptTemplates.title: Feature with Clarification
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to add a feature to [FILE]. The feature should [DESCRIPTION]. There are some aspects I am unsure about: [LIST_QUESTIONS].
promptTemplates.scenario: When implementing a feature with ambiguous requirements

promptTemplates.title: Pattern-Based Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement the new [FEATURE] following the existing patterns in the [DIRECTORY] directory. Suggest a quick validation step to confirm it works correctly.
promptTemplates.scenario: When adding features that should match existing code patterns

promptTemplates.title: Safe Modification Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Make a minimal change to [FILE] to [GOAL]. Review the change and propose a validation command.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a small, focused code change with verification

bestPractices: Start with small, reviewable patches instead of large refactors
Ask clarifying questions when requirements are unclear before writing code
Use existing repository patterns and utilities instead of introducing new dependencies
Always propose a validation step when changing code behavior
antiPatterns: Making large, sweeping changes without breaking them into smaller steps
Implementing features without confirming understanding of requirements
Adding new dependencies when existing utilities would work
Skipping validation steps when behavior changes

faq.question: What makes this skill different from a standard coding assistant?
faq.answer: This skill specifically emphasizes safe, incremental code changes. It prioritizes small diffs, clarity, and validation over rapid implementation. It also explicitly asks clarifying questions when requirements are ambiguous.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No, the skill suggests validation steps like unit tests or build commands but does not execute them. It relies on the developer or CI system to run actual tests.

faq.question: How does this skill handle ambiguous requirements?
faq.answer: The skill explicitly instructs to ask 1-3 clarifying questions before implementing when requirements are unclear. This prevents wasted effort on incorrect implementations.

faq.question: What kinds of changes does this skill prefer?
faq.answer: The skill prefers minimal, reviewable patches. It favors existing repository patterns and utilities over new dependencies. It also asks for explicit user approval before any destructive actions.

faq.question: Can this skill help with test-driven development?
faq.answer: The skill can suggest validation steps including tests when behavior changes. However, it does not generate comprehensive test suites from scratch or enforce TDD methodology.

faq.question: What tools does this skill work with?
faq.answer: This skill supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It provides guidance that works across different AI coding assistants.
